supporting_evidence: state.applyUpdate (seq=20) records a committed expanded transition from target item E9905B91-621C-4DA3-AF30-EF8E0EE9C6B0 to nil before the animation batch.
Collapse path executes animation.schedule -> animation.start -> animation.batch.begin/end -> animation.complete for the same affected ID, while surrounding row positions animate.
contradicting_evidence: Discriminating probe during collapse logs border geometry jumping from detailVisibleHeight=620.333 (probeSample=1) to detailVisibleHeight=0.000 (probeSample=2) while animating=true and animation.batch is active, indicating snap behavior inside rendering/interpolation.
Probe rowToken remains stable (7CE9E103-25DA-4BB9-A888-799842D6E1D1) across samples in the transition window, which argues against row view identity recreation as the primary cause.
missing_evidence: Which exact border/clip parameter in the row view lacks animatable interpolation across the collapse/edit transition.
candidates.next_discriminating_check: Inspect the row border rendering path and log the animatable value(s) that drive the bottom-border/detail reveal each frame during collapse to identify the specific non-interpolating parameter.
